description_long: This object is a roughly cylindrical stone with a weathered, irregular surface and visible cracks running vertically and horizontally. The top is uneven and slightly domed, with some areas appearing more eroded or chipped than others. One side of the stone reveals a more exposed, rougher interior texture, suggesting partial breakage or natural wear. The coloration is predominantly a pale grayish-white, with some areas showing darker, earthier tones, especially where the stone is more exposed. The surface is matte and rough, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. This stone would likely serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park, garden, or along a pathway.
description: A weathered, cylindrical stone with a rough, cracked surface and uneven top, showing both pale and earthy tones. One side is more eroded, revealing a coarse interior.
description_view_0: The stone appears mostly intact and cylindrical, with visible cracks and a slightly domed, uneven top.
description_view_1: This view highlights the vertical cracks and the overall cylindrical shape, with the surface appearing more uniformly pale and less eroded.
description_view_2: A large, rough, and eroded section is visible, exposing the stone's coarse interior and contrasting with the smoother outer surface.
description_view_3: The most eroded side is prominent, with deep recesses and a mix of pale and earthy tones, showing significant weathering.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier along a walkway
attribute_pattern: Irregular cracking and mottling, with some natural veining and exposed rough patches; no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Cracked and eroded, with significant surface roughness and visible signs of natural aging.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured or paved surfaces, but can blend with natural landscaping or other stones; may accumulate dirt or moss at the base.
